title: Composing Legacy Synthesis
summary: Integrate legacy synthesis artifacts into KBv2.4 using key-based data partitioning to enhance scalable query pruning and optimize data retrieval.
standard: Ensure seamless integration of legacy artifacts with modern data partitioning techniques for improved scalability and performance.
procedure: Execute legacy artifact conversion using KBv2.4 tools to standardize formats following the defined protocol with boundary checks; assert exit_code < 1 and success_indicator = true; emit completion_event on success.

Execute key-based partitioning by hashing primary keys and distributing across partitions by executing the transformation function on each input record; assert output_schema_valid = true and error_count < 1; log transformation_duration_ms.

Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on converted data to identify query bottlenecks with automated substep verification; assert substep_pass_count = substep_total and error_count < 1; persist results before advancing.

Invoke partition pruning to eliminate non-relevant partitions during query execution by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Assert merged output by comparing query results with expected outcomes by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Assign results using automated scripts to ensure data integrity and consistency using a deterministic decision matrix: evaluate each criterion in priority order; assign first matching class or UNCLASSIFIED; assert confidence_score >= 0.7 for all classified records.
failure_modes: Increased query latency due to improper partitioning.
Data inconsistency from incorrect artifact conversion; this occurs when the triggering event is not handled at the correct boundary, causing the condition to surface in a downstream stage.
Scalability issues from unoptimized key distribution; this occurs when required context is unavailable at execution time, causing the step to proceed with incomplete information.
verification: Confirm p95 query time is reduced by 30% post-integration; assert integration_test_pass_rate >= 1.0 and incompatibility_count < 1.
Ensure data consistency checks pass with 100% accuracy; assert accuracy_score >= 0.95 and false_positive_rate < 0.05.
Verify partition distribution is balanced across nodes; assert output_valid = true and error_count < 1.
